item.title: Swim season
item.description: With the warm days, swim season is upon us in Stockholm! I went swimming every day since last Saturday, it is glorious. The water in the sea was still quite cold, around 13-14 degrees. In Brunnsviken though, it is perfect at around 18-19 degrees !

item.title: appearances
item.description: 2021 2021-06-07 - Journées de la SF2A, online 2021-04-19 - 2021 STScI Spring Symposium, online 2021-04-12 - BCool meeting 2021, online 2021-03-02 - Cool Stars 20.5 conference, online 2020 2020-12-01 - The magnetic field awakens conference, online 2020-10-06 - Launch of the Thunberg fellowship programme at Swedish Collegium for Advanced Study, Uppsala (Sweden) 2020-05-26 - Public PhD defense at Polhemsalen, Ångströmlaboratoriet, Uppsala University (Sweden) 2019 2019.

item.title: Hops growing
item.description: The hops planted early May are growing fast and seriously climbing that pole.

item.title: Travailler
item.description: Today it was difficult to get into the mood for efficient work. Lots of things happening, 1+ year of Covid, and a bright warm sunny day in Stockholm. My mood took me into listening to some “summer tunes”, which for me are ska/reggae/punk songs. I particularly listened to these two gems about working (travailler in French) less. Tryo - Travailler plus. Tryo is a staple of French music, particularly among (hippie) people in my generation, with some great hit songs [1, 2, 3].

item.title: Bookmark clean-up II
item.description: I have recently gone through my bookmarks and cleaned them up. In the process I found some hidden gems, here is a second short collection of links, with a music theme (and even some Eurovision). 🇱🇻 Latvia - Babite Mixed Choir Maska, great performance at Eurovision choir 2019 🇸🇮 zalagasper - S teboi, a band who performed another song at Eurovision 2019 🇳🇴 Aurora - Full Performance (Live on KEXP), some fresh music live on the great radio KEXP 🇮🇸 amiina - Full Performance (Live on KEXP), a relaxing performance, also live on KEXP Dvorak : “New World” Symphony No.

item.title: A year of records
item.description: Around a year ago, I defended my PhD thesis 🥳! To celebrate, my kind colleagues offered me a record player. Since then, I bought quite some records, second-hand when possible, discogs has been a great source in these pandemics time. When I was younger, I had cassettes, then CDs. When I got access to internet, I started using P2P, torrents, and streaming services such as Grooveshark, Spotify, or Deezer. Having now this record player is a welcomed return to playing music on a physical medium.

item.title: A voté
item.description: Yesterday I biked to the French embassy in Stockholm to vote for conseillers consulaires, consular advisers. Their mandate is to vote in the elections for the French senate, advise French policy-makers on matters affecting French citizens abroad, and provide help to the French citizens abroad. These elections are rather low-key, but I realized that it is one of the election where my vote has the largest influence: probably around 2000 people voted in total.

item.title: Catalan humour - Manuel Valls
item.description: Manuel Valls, a man you cannot trust, is coming back to France after a short stint in Barcelona politics, eyeing the next French presidential elections. Catalans seem happy to get rid of him. The video is a cover of this infamous song.

item.title: Forest flowers
item.description: Spring is now definitely here 🥳!

item.title: Bookmark clean-up
item.description: I haven’t gone thoroughly through my browser bookmarks in around a decade, and it was time to rummage and do a clean-up. The first realization is that around a third of all pages bookmarked do not exist anymore. This made me think about webpages longevity in general. The second realization is that there are tons of great random links in there that I just never went back to visit. Here are a few:

item.title: cv
item.description: Here you can download my academic CV (PDF document) The list of my publications can be found at this link (redirects to the ADS). Short CV Research expertise Stellar magnetic fields: Zeeman broadening and Zeeman Doppler Imaging High-resolution spectroscopy and spectropolarimetry, optical and near-infrared Astronomical instrumentation Research positions 🇸🇪 Thunberg fellow, Uppsala University & Swedish Collegium for Advanced Study, Sep 2020 - 🇸🇪 🇩🇪 PhD student, Uppsala University & European Southern Observatory, Oct 2013 - May 2020 🇳🇱 ESA Stagiaire, European Space Agency ESTEC, Netherlands, Mar 2013 - Jun 2013 Education 🇸🇪 🇩🇪 PhD in astronomy at Uppsala University.

item.title: Typography
item.description: Typography is one of my nerdy hobbies. I do like a nicely printed book or magasine in a great typeface, and the same works for electronic content. There are so many high-quality free fonts around these days, that I changed the fonts used on this website many (many) times already. Now, in a low-tech effort, I decided to switch to default system fonts. This way, your browser does not download extra font-files when you browse here.

item.title: Standing desk
item.description: For a few years back, I have been doing some work from home, and bought a standard IKEA manual standing desk for the home office. This was a great buy, allowing to have good ergonomy while working at home. When the Covid pandemic started, we ended up being three people working from home, and I wanted a second desk where I could be standing. I decided to buy a Standsome slim, which I got to try by chance in the public library in Karlsruhe where I stopped to work a day during a train trip.

item.title: Les misérables
item.description: I recently re-read Les Misérables from Victor Hugo. As a Frenchperson, it is of course a must-read, having had to idolize Victor Hugo all through schoolyears and beyond. I am not the biggest fan, but I must say that the guy can write. Some sentences just resonate with me. À ses pieds ce qu’on peut cultiver et cueillir ; sur sa tête ce qu’on peut étudier et méditer; quelques fleurs sur la terre et toutes les étoiles dans le ciel.

item.title: Kungsängslilja
item.description: A pretty picture today, with one of my favourite flower: kungsängslilja in Swedish (and a great name in French: Fritillaire pintade). It is the official flower of Uppland province, which encompasses Uppsala.

item.title: Lappis humlegården
item.description: As a homebrewer, I am interested in the four basic ingredients that we combine to make beer: water malt hops yeast The tap water in Stockholm comes from lake Mälaren and is excellent. Malt I can buy locally from Swedish maltsters (e.g Warbro Kvarn). Amazing yeasts can be found too, like the Norwegian kveik yeast that can be dryed & re-used from batch to batch. What about hops though?

item.title: Covid
item.description: Well, I am sick this week, so nothing to be seen here. Come on, move along :-).

item.title: It's a start
item.description: Welcome to this blog! Hello world I guess. Why this blog? Well, thanks for asking. You provide some sort of peer-pressure so that I regularly write something. I suck at writing, and I dislike it. So this will be a good kick in the butt. Expect some science, much nonsense, some landscapes, beer-brewing and other misc. How often should I write? How often will I write? Who knows … But at least a post a week should be good, non?

item.title: Cool Stars 20.5
item.description: 2021-03-02 🠖 2021-03-04, online Conference website Interactive poster PDF poster 🠔 back to presentations

item.title: about
item.description: Who am I? I grew up in Southern France, where the Pyrénées mountain range meet the Mediterranean sea, also where there is the biggest oak barrel in the world. I studied in Perpignan 🇫🇷, Marseille 🇫🇷, Uppsala 🇸🇪, Paris/Meudon/Orsay 🇫🇷. I shortly worked at the European Space Agency in Noordwijk 🇳🇱, qualifying the Descent Camera for the Exomars 2016 lander (which made an impressive crater on Mars) before completing a PhD in astronomy at Uppsala University 🇸🇪 with a 1-year studentship at the European Southern Observatory in Garching near München 🇩🇪🍺.

item.title: Launch of the Thunberg Fellowship Programme
item.description: 2020-10-06 at the Swedish Collegium for Advanced Study, Uppsala (Sweden) Slides from my presentation 🠔 back to presentations

item.title: research
item.description: Research interests Transit spectroscopy and exoplanetary atmospheres Magnetism of cool stars High-resolution near-infrared spectroscopy High-resolution optical spectropolarimetry and Zeeman-Doppler imaging Instrumentation Refereed publications The large-scale magnetic field of the eccentric pre-main-sequence binary system V1878 Ori. Lavail, A., Kochukhov, O., Hussain, G., et al. 2020, MNRAS, 497, 632. ADS | DOI Characterising the surface magnetic fields of T Tauri stars with high-resolution near-infrared spectroscopy. Lavail, A.

item.title: PhD defense
item.description: 2020-05-26 at Uppsala University (Sweden) The day I got my PhD, also my birthday if you want to know. Slides from my presentation 🠔 back to presentations

item.title: AD Leo. Observations from 2019
item.description: LSD Profiles We got 6 observations in 2019. Stokes V profiles shown below. It looks like amplitude-wise, the profiles are recovered most of the pre-2016 amplitude. Stokes V profiles The phase coverage is not great, we have four observations clustered at phase 0.7-0.8, one at 0.0, one around 0.5: 0.00 0.46 2.72 1.80 1.81 1.81 ZDI I ran ZDI on the data anyhow, following same approach than for 2012 and 2016 data, with the same Stokes I filling factor value, regularisation parameters values, and Stokes V filling factor values.

item.description: 2019-10-24 🠖 2019-10-26, at Stockholm University (Sweden) Conference website PDF poster 🠔 back to presentations

item.title: A logo proposal for Svenska astronomiska sällskapet
contact
item.description: If you’d like to get in touch / have any question, feel free to contact me! E-mail LinkedIn Visiting adress: room Å95123, Ångströmlaboratoriet, Lägerhyddsvägen 1, Uppsala Post adress: Alexis Lavail, Box 516, 75120 Uppsala (Sweden)

item.title: Presentations from the Uppsala-Stockholm PhD days
item.description: 10-11 april 2014, Uppsala Universitet Thursday 10 april 2014 Kai Yan Lee - Reionization and the cosmic dawn Hannes Jensen - Simulating observational probes of reionization Saghar Asadi - Gravitational millilensing as a probe of dark matter on subgalactic scales Beatriz Villarroel Rodriguez - What is an Active Galactic Nuclei? Sarah Jabbari - Origin of solar surface activity and sunspots Alexis Lavail - Investigation of stellar magnetic fields using infrared spectropolarimetry Lisa Rosén - Magnetic fields of cool active stars Naum Rusomarov - Magnetic fields of Ap stars from all four Stokes parameters Friday 11 april 2014 Andreas Sandberg - Observations of Lyman alpha emitting galaxies at low and intermediate redshifts Florent Duval - Lyman alpha radiative transfer in Star-burst Galaxies Johannes Puschnig - Multi-wavelength studies of star-forming galaxies Thøger Juul Rivera-Thorsen - Neutral gas kinematics and geometry in young, starforming galaxies and its impact on Lyman alpha radiation transport Samuel Regandell - Spectral Synthesis for protoplanetary disk models Gianni Cataldi - Observations and modeling of debris disks Sara Lindgren - Accurate spectroscopy of M dwarfs, a possible link to understand planet formation Erik Aronson - Spectroscopic characterization of exoplanetary atmospheres

item.title: STARRY conference
item.description: 2019-06-18 🠖 2019-06-21 in Leeds (UK) Conference website Slides from my presentation 🠔 back to presentations

item.title: A sudden change of the global magnetic field of the active M-dwarf AD Leo
item.description: Alexis Lavail, Oleg Kochukhov, and Gregg Wade A poster presented at the Cool Stars 20 conference Useful links PDF version of the poster Link to the paper on ADS Summary of the science in not-so-rigorous language Our aim was to get the first observations ever of a M-dwarf in all four Stokes parameters. This means one gets the intensity spectrum (Stokes I), the circularly polarized spectrum (Stokes V) and the two linearly polarized spectra (Stokes Q and U).

item.title: Cool Stars 20
